governing_logic: Nomdara is not emitted as a land kingdom. It is emitted as mobile connective governance: convoy law, flotillas, portable archives, hearing camps, and treaty circuits.
core_assumptions: reaches difficult maritime and cold-route interfaces
functions as connective trade diplomacy and neutral legal tissue
grows through organized mobility classes and portable institutions rather than permanent sovereign towns

role_bands.function: Creates portable legality through convoy edicts, custody logs, bonded witness chains, onboard hearings, mobile registries, and neutral enforcement customs.
Turns motion into diplomacy through interpreters, corridor envoys, exchange witnesses, treaty relays, and temporary oath tables capable of convening where fixed states cannot.
Runs portable markets, weighted exchange, bonded cargo holds, emergency barter balancing, and inter-polity load transfer at difficult interfaces.
Makes hard routes survivable through weather reading, cold-route discipline, storm avoidance, escort scheduling, salvage rights, and relief planning.
Absorbs tension without full escalation through rumor suppression, side-cargo separation, quarantine holding, neutral camp zoning, and discreet high-friction handoffs.

node_allocations.functional_profile: Mobile deliberative seat where caravan law, seals, and portable hearing authority travel with the polity.
Rotating commercial backbone that links disparate markets through scheduled portable exchange.
Storm-capable flotilla for frozen and semi-frozen northern maritime mediation.
Warm-sea convoy group specialized in hurricane-prone southern movement and emergency redirection.
Heat, medicine, replacement parts, and ration carriage into dome-survival zones.
Portable balancing chain between industrial surplus, agricultural need, and route law.
Specialized coastal mediator for hot cargo, forge exports, and ash-weather disruption.
Traveling diplomatic lane that turns roads into temporary embassy ground.
Portable arbitration camp for trade, cargo, and bond disputes outside settled halls.
Regulated lecture and copy-bearing caravan for controlled knowledge circulation.
Protected movement corridor for mortuary, offering, and sacred-travel cargo.
Floating copy-transfer and record custody node linking maritime archives to moving exchange law.
